titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5953

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=790 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 13:36:57.8491+00 | translation moa=791 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 13:36:57.858958+00

div.p:
Omnibus Christi fidelibus has litteras uisuris uel audituris . Bertramus filius Ade filii Cospatricii de Parua Ristona Salutem . Nouerit uniuersitas uestra
me intuitu caritatis et pro salute anime mee et Margarete uxoris mee . et pro salute patris mei . et matris mee . et Roberti filii mei
et predecessorum et successorum meorum dedisse . concessisse . et hac presenti carta confirmasse deo et sancte Marie . et promocioni
Ecclesie de Coldingham in puram et perpetuam elemosinam . nouem acras terre in territorio Parue Ristone . Scilicet quatuor acras in Blackechet’ iuxta
Farnebed . et quinque acras in Goselauflat . tenendas et abendas de me et heredibus meis in puram et perpetuam elemosinam inperpetuam . cum
communem pastura et omnibus libertatibus et aysiamentis ad tantam terram infra uillam et extra pertinentibus . Quare uolo et concedo ut
predicta ecclesia prefata nouem acras terre habeat et in perpetuum possideat . libere . quiete . et absolute ab omnibus operibus seruiciis secularibus
auxiliis exactionibus et demandis . Ego uero et heredes mei prefatas nouem acras terre sepe dicte ecclesie contra omnes homines et feminas
uuarantizabimus . Et ut mea donacio rata et inconcussa semper permaneat huic scripto sigillum meum apposui . Hiis testibus  Domino
. A . priore tunc temporis de Coldingham . Domino Willelmo de Mordingtona . Domino Alano de Suintona . Alano filio eius . Ada de Prenderguest .
Willelmo de Scremerstona . Willelmo uicario de Aldecambus . Iohanne filio Helie . Ada tunc marescallo . Robertus filio Gregorii Senescaldi et
multis aliis .

p.cl: To all Christ's faithful who shall view or hear these letters, Bertram, son of Adam son of Cospatric of Little Reston, sends Greeting.

moved by charity and for the salvation of my own soul, and of my wife Margaret's, and for the salvation of my father and my mother, and of Robert my son, and my predecessors and successors, to have given, granted, and to have made firm by this present charter, to God and to Saint Mary, and for the improvement of the church of Coldingham, in pure and perpetual alms, nine acres of land in the territory of Little Reston, that is, four acres in Blackechet' near Farnebed, and five acres in Goselauflat, to be held and had of me and my heirs in pure and everlasting alms for ever, with common pasture and all liberties and easements pertaining to so much land inside and outside the toun. Wherefore I will and grant that the aforesaid church should have and possess the aforementioned nine acres of land for ever, free, quit, and released from all works, secular services, aids, exactions and demands. And I and my heirs shall warrant the aforementioned nine acres of land to the oft-mentioned church against all men and women.
And so that my donation should ever remain valid and undisputed, I have applied my seal to this writing.

With these witnesses: the lord Anketin, then prior of Coldingham; the lord William of Mordington; the lord Alan of Swinton; Alan his son; Adam of Prenderguest; William of Scremerston; William, vicar of Oldcambus; John son of Elias; Adam, then marischal; Robert, son of Gregory the Steward; and many others.
